title: Cattle & Customer Management System — Full Rebuild with Admin and Customer Portals

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model and CRUD operations for Cattle records with the following fields: tagNumber (Text), breed (Text), dateOfPurchase (Int/timestamp), milkingCapacity (Float, litres/day), purchasePrice (Float), availability (variant: Available | Sold | Reserved), healthStatus (variant: Healthy | Sick | Recovered). Expose addCattle, updateCattle, deleteCattle, and getAllCattle functions. Only the admin principal can call mutating functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can add a cattle record with all seven required fields
Admin can edit any field of an existing cattle record
Admin can delete a cattle record by ID
All cattle records are returned via getAllCattle
Non-admin callers receive an authorization error on mutating calls

requirements.text: Implement a backend data model and CRUD operations for Customer accounts with fields: name (Text), phone (Text), address (Text), username (Text, unique login credential), passwordHash (Text), isActive (Bool). Expose addCustomer, updateCustomer, deleteCustomer, setCustomerActive, and getAllCustomers functions. Only the admin principal can call these functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can add a new customer with all required fields
Admin can edit customer details
Admin can delete a customer account
Admin can toggle a customer's isActive status
getAllCustomers returns all customer records to the admin

requirements.text: Implement customer authentication backend logic: a customerLogin(username, password) function that checks credentials against stored customer accounts, returns a session token or error if the account does not exist or isActive is false. Inactive and non-existent customers must be explicitly rejected with a clear error variant.
acceptanceCriteria: customerLogin succeeds for admin-created, active customers with correct credentials
customerLogin returns an error for unknown usernames
customerLogin returns an error for inactive customers even if credentials are correct
customerLogin returns an error for incorrect passwords

requirements.text: Implement backend Order management: Order record with fields orderId (Nat), customerId (Nat), cattleTagNumber (Text), orderDate (Int), status (variant: Pending | Confirmed | OutForDelivery | Delivered | Cancelled), deliveryNotes (Text). Expose placeOrder (customer-callable, only for own orders), updateOrderStatus (admin-callable), getAllOrders (admin-callable), getMyOrders (customer-callable, returns only the caller's orders).
acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated active customers can place orders
Admin can view all orders
Admin can update order status through all status variants
Customers can only view their own orders via getMyOrders
Order records persist across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Build the Admin Panel frontend with a protected login screen (username/password). After login, provide navigation to three sections: (1) Cattle Management — table listing all cattle with tag number, breed, date of purchase, milking capacity, purchase price, availability, health status, and action buttons for add/edit/delete; (2) Customer Management — table listing all customers with name, phone, address, active/inactive status toggle, and add/edit/delete actions; (3) Orders & Deliveries — table of all orders with customer name, cattle tag, order date, status, and ability to update delivery status.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin login screen is shown to unauthenticated users navigating to /admin
Correct admin credentials grant access to the admin panel
Cattle table displays all fields and supports add/edit/delete via dialogs
Customer table supports add/edit/delete and active/inactive toggle
Orders table displays all orders and allows status updates
Admin is redirected to login on logout or session expiry

requirements.text: Build the Customer Portal frontend at /portal with a username/password login form. After successful login, show two tabs: (1) Place Order — allows the customer to submit a new order by selecting available cattle (shown by tag number and breed) and entering delivery notes; (2) My Deliveries — lists the customer's own orders with status badges (Pending, Confirmed, Out for Delivery, Delivered, Cancelled) and delivery notes. Block access entirely for inactive or non-existent customer accounts and show a clear 'Access denied' message.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer login form is shown at /portal for unauthenticated users
Active, admin-created customers can log in with correct credentials
Inactive customers see an 'Access denied / account inactive' message after login attempt
Place Order tab shows available cattle and submits an order
My Deliveries tab shows only the logged-in customer's orders with current statuses
Customers cannot access cattle inventory details beyond tag number and breed for ordering

requirements.text: Apply a consistent visual theme across both the Admin Panel and Customer Portal: use a clean, professional green-and-white color palette with earthy accent tones, clear typography, and card-based layouts. Admin panel should feel authoritative and data-dense; customer portal should feel friendly and simple.
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette is applied consistently across all pages
Admin panel and customer portal share a coherent but role-appropriate visual style
All tables, forms, and dialogs use the defined theme

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in a single main actor (backend/main.mo)
Do not use Internet Identity for customer authentication — use username/password stored in the canister
Customers must not be able to self-register; only admin-created accounts are valid
Inactive customers must be blocked from all customer portal access
Immutable frontend paths (hooks, main.tsx, ui components) must not be modified
nonGoals: Social login or Internet Identity for customers
Customers browsing the full cattle inventory
Monthly reports or CSV export features
